violations.text: The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview with staff, Casserole was placed hot in the walk-in, observed tightly wrapped with plastic. Plastic wrap was removed to facilitate cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The door to the outside, located in the dining area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by small flies at the downstairs bar. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in and bar. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Staff placed utensils in crock with hot water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

Utensils (cook ware and silverware) are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machines. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The area surrounding the dumpster was observed with foam bricks and old equipment. Dumpster areas must be maintained free of debris to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the dining area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings under exterior stairs and dumpster area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC stated PCS is actively working to eliminate issue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2024

The walk-in cooler located area was not in good repair, observed with ambient temperature of 63. Technician was on-site at the time of inspection, repairs were completed and awaiting temperature to return to 41 degrees or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The kitchen prep sink is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The floor and wall junctures in the walk-in do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The wall in the walk-in is in poor repair. Inspector observed loose FRP. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed within facility and outdoor area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024
Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
Several hand washing sinks lack proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The door to the outside, located in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on patio and dumpster area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
Per PIC, walk-in cooler is in need of service. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of rubbish and debris. Refuse areas must be maintained to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The floor and wall junctures in the upstairs bar storage area and kitchen areas do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2024
The wall and doorway to the kitchen is unfinished. Walls must be constructed of a material that is smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
